extra.reasoning: slurred_speech appears in only one rule, r2 (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity) and requires chest_pain AND palpitations AND hemoptysis AND rash AND slurred_speech. Turning slurred_speech on can therefore only ever enable r2, which can lower acuity. Take the input chest_pain=T, palpitations=T, hemoptysis=T, rash=T, dyspnea=F. With slurred_speech=FALSE, r2 does not fire; the matching rules are r1 (urgent, p2), r6 (urgent, p2), r3 (emergent, p3), r4 (emergent, p3). The highest priority is 3, giving acuity = emergent. With slurred_speech=TRUE (all else unchanged), r2 now fires at priority 100 (beating priority 3) and sets acuity = self_care. emergent -> self_care is a decrease in the ordered enum (self_care < routine < urgent < emergent), so setting slurred_speech true lowers acuity. The monotonicity property is violated. The witness_assignment is the input (with slurred_speech=true) whose result self_care is lower than the result emergent obtained from the same input with slurred_speech=false.
